Vaultz Capital stock rises with US cross-trading launch
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Vaultz Capital PLC on Friday began trading on the OTCQB Venture Market, as the bitcoin-focused firm courts US investors.

Vaultz Capital is a London-based operator of digital infrastructure for bitcoin mining, which trades on the Aquis Exchange.

Its shares were quoted 13% higher at 4.25 pence around midday on Friday on Aquis.

It was approved for a dual listing in Frankfurt at the end of September.

Separately, the OTC Markets Group has approved cross-trading of Vaultz shares in the US, following an application by the company in July.

Vaultz said that the OTC listing will not impact shares trading on Aquis or in Frankfurt, nor will it be issuing any new shares as part of the cross-trading launch.

Chief Executive Eric Benz commented: "Our admission to the OTCQB represents another significant milestone in Vaultz's growth journey. It provides US investors with an accessible platform to trade our shares and enables us to broaden our global investor base. We believe this step will enhance the company's visibility, improve share liquidity and support our long-term capital strategy."

By Holly Munks, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

data.title: Lifco Business Area Manager Martin Linder Purchases Shares Worth SEK 2.1 Million
data.description:
Martin Linder, head of the Systems Solutions business area at industrial conglomerate Lifco, acquired 5,500 B shares in the company on October 24. The shares were purchased at a price of SEK 374.96 each, amounting to a total transaction value of SEK 2.1 million. The purchase was executed on Nasdaq Stockholm, according to disclosures from the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority's insider registry.

Following this transaction, Linder now holds a total of 273,000 shares in Lifco, according to ownership data from Holdings.

Glenfarne Alaska LNG, LLC and Tokyo Gas Co., Ltd. Announce the Signing of Letter of Intent for the Offtake of Liquefied Natural Gas from the Alaska LNG Project
data.description:
Glenfarne Alaska LNG, LLC and Tokyo Gas Co. Ltd. announced the signing of a Letter of Intent (LOI) for the offtake of one million tonnes per annum (MTPA) of liquefied natural gas (LNG) from the Alaska LNG project, majority owned and developed by Glenfarne. This LOI adds commercial momentum to Glenfarne's rapid progress developing the 20 MTPA project, the only federally authorized export terminal on the U.S. Pacific Coast.

Since becoming lead developer of Alaska LNG in March 2025, Glenfarne has signed preliminary offtake agreements with leading LNG buyers in Japan, Korea, Taiwan, and Thailand that includes JERA, POSCO, CPC, and PTT, totaling 11 MTPA of capacity of the 16 MTPA Glenfarne expects to contract to reach a financial close for the project. In addition to Glenfarne's rapidly growing portfolio of Alaska LNG offtake partners, Worley is completing the final engineering and cost validation for the project's 807-mile pipeline. Alaska LNG consists of a 42-inch diameter pipeline to transport natural gas from Alaska's North Slope to meet Alaska's domestic needs and produce 20 MTPA of LNG for export.

Glenfarne is developing Alaska LNG in two financially independent phases to accelerate project execution. Phase One includes the domestic pipeline to deliver natural gas approximately 765 miles from the North Slope to the Anchorage region. Phase Two will add the LNG terminal and related infrastructure to enable export capability.

The State of Alaska, through the Alaska Gasline Development Corporation, owns 25% of Alaska LNG.

Gotion High-tech Co.,Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Gotion High-tech Co.,Ltd. reported earnings results for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the nine months, the company reported sales was CNY 29,507.98 million compared to CNY 25,174.85 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 29,507.98 million compared to CNY 25,174.85 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 2,533.23 million compared to CNY 412.34 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.4 compared to CNY 0.23 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.4 compared to CNY 0.23 a year ago.

data.title: General Dynamics 3Q Revenue Rises on Stronger Business-Jet Demand -- Update
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller


General Dynamics posted higher third-quarter revenue and profit, driven by increased orders for business jets as corporate profits continue to grow and a new tax law offered companies incentives to buy planes.

Sales rose 11% to $12.91 billion from the previous quarter, beating Wall Street's expectation of $12.5 billion. Top-line gains were fueled by a 30% increase in aerospace revenue, which Chief Executive Phebe Novakovic partially attributed to "very strong" order activity for business jets.

Seaport analyst Richard Safran said in a note last month that demand for business jets would likely continue to grow as President Trump's policies help grow corporate profits and a "super-wealthy class." Trump's tax bill signed earlier this year also allows businesses to deduct the full cost of a new or used business jet in the first year of acquisition. "We think that spurs increased demand from both existing and new customers," Safran said.

The U.S. government also has significant levels of unspent defense funding, which should offer a significant boost to General Dynamics' defense business as the Trump administration attempts to "pull-forward as much production as industry will facilitate," Safran said.

For the third quarter, the Reston, Va., aerospace-and-defense company on Friday reported net income of $1.06 billion, or $3.88 a share, up from $930 million, or $3.35 a share the year prior. Analysts had expected $3.71 a share, according to FactSet.

The company said its total estimated contract value was $167.7 billion at the end of the quarter, including a backlog of $109.9 billion.

Shares were up 2.1% to $348.55 in premarket trading.


Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0748ET

data.title: Xali Gold Acquires Pico Machay Gold Project in Peru for US$17.5 Million
HCA Lifts Outlook as 3Q Profit, Revenue Rise
data.description:
By Connor Hart


HCA Healthcare lifted its full-year outlook after logging higher profit and revenue in the third quarter, as same-facility admissions grew.

The Nashville, Tenn., hospital owner and operation said earnings are now on track to come in between $6.5 billion and $6.72 billion, or $27 to $28 a share. It had previously guided for a profit of $6.11 billion to $6.48 billion, or $25.50 to $27 a share.

The company now expects revenue of $75 billion to $76.5 billion, up from its prior outlook of $74 billion to $76 billion. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast earnings of $26.33 on revenue of $75 billion.

The new outlook came as HCA posted a profit of $1.64 billion, or $6.96 a share, for its three months ended Sept. 30, compared with $1.27 billion, or $4.88 a share, in the same quarter a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, adjusted earnings were also $6.96 a share. Analysts were looking for $5.72 a share.

Revenue rose 9.6% to $19.16 billion, topping the $18.57 billion that Wall Street modeled.

Same-facility admissions increased 2.1% during the quarter. Chief Executive Sam Hazen said the company is continuing its work to increase access to healthcare, invest in advanced technologies and train its workers.

Shares rose 2% to $449.05 in premarket trading.


Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0748ET
